skill.name: image-assistant
skill.description: Turns articles and modules into clear 16:9 infographic prompt packs. It guides brief, image planning, copy compression, prompt packaging, and optional APIMart batch generation.
skill.summary: Create concise, readable infographic prompts from long-form content.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical heuristic combinations, but review found no evidence of prompt injection, hidden obfuscation, or malicious exfiltration. The confirmed risk is an optional APIMart batch script that reads local configuration, sends prompts to an external image API with a bearer token, downloads returned image URLs, and writes run metadata. Publish with a clear warning that API mode sends prompt content to a third party and requires local token handling.

medium_findings.title: External API Call With Local Bearer Token
medium_findings.description: The batch generation script reads a local TOKEN value and sends prompt payloads to a configurable APIMart image endpoint using an Authorization bearer header. This is an intended feature, but prompt content and the token are exposed to the configured network service when API mode is used.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs an Authorization bearer header and posts JSON to the configured API URL. The behavior is documented as an optional generation feature, so the risk is confirmed but not malicious.

medium_findings.title: Downloads URLs Returned By External Service
medium_findings.description: The script recursively extracts HTTP URLs from API responses and downloads them into the output directory. This can fetch arbitrary URLs returned by the configured service, so users should only run it with trusted API endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL extraction and download paths are explicit in code. The source of the URLs is the API response, which makes legitimate use likely but still creates a meaningful network and file-write risk.

medium_findings.title: API Mode Executes After User Selects Batch Option
medium_findings.description: The stage instructions say to run the batch API flow after the user chooses that mode, without another confirmation after JSONL creation. This is not hidden execution, but it can surprise users if prompt contents include sensitive material.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow text explicitly describes batch API execution after the user selects the API option. The prior user choice lowers severity, but the lack of a final confirmation is a real operational concern.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are markdown code fences, path references, or template references rather than code that executes automatically. They document how to run the optional local script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown tables, fenced commands, and usage documentation. They do not create an execution path unless a user chooses to run the script.

low_findings.title: Base64 Decode Used For Image Response Handling
low_findings.description: The base64 decoding path processes image blobs returned by the image API and writes image files. No evidence found that it decodes hidden instructions or executable payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The decoded data is treated as image bytes and saved with an image extension. This matches normal image API response handling.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Markdown Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high entropy findings point to normal markdown content with Chinese text, templates, and prompt wording. No evidence found of encrypted payloads, hidden binary content, or evasion text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable markdown instructions and design templates. The entropy signal appears caused by multilingual text and dense prompt language.

low_findings.title: Configuration File Placeholder Does Not Contain A Secret
low_findings.description: The bundled APIMart config says local tokens should not be shared, but the TOKEN field is empty in the reviewed file. This is a documentation hygiene issue, not credential leakage in the package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file includes an empty TOKEN value and a placeholder template. The warning text is appropriate, but shipping a real-looking env filename can encourage unsafe local edits.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configurable Network Endpoint Receives Authorization Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The API URL comes from local configuration with a default APIMart endpoint, and the same request includes a bearer token. If the config is changed to an untrusted endpoint, the token would be sent there.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is clear from the code and is a known token-handling risk. It depends on user-controlled local configuration, so it is not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt And Response Metadata Written To Run File
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes prompts, request payloads, response headers, task results, and image URLs to run.json. This can persist sensitive prompt text locally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The run file structure explicitly stores request and response metadata. This is useful for debugging but should be documented because prompts may contain private content.

content.value_statement: Long articles are hard to turn into readable visuals without overloading each image. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through planning, copy compression, prompt writing, and optional batch image generation.
actual_capabilities: Clarifies audience, use case, viewing distance, and text density before planning images.
Splits long content into a recommended list of images and layout templates.
Creates Copy Spec drafts with exact on-image text and text budgets.
Packages final 16:9 prompts with style, layout, readability, and negative constraints.
Provides optional APIMart JSONL templates and a local batch generation script.
limitations: It does not generate images unless the optional API script is configured and run.
The default visual style is optimized for Chinese 16:9 infographic prompts.
Generated image quality still depends on the selected image model.
API mode sends prompt content to the configured external service.

use_cases.title: Turn Articles Into Visual Briefs
use_cases.description: Convert a long article section into a small set of readable infographic concepts and prompts.

use_cases.title: Prepare Teaching Slides
use_cases.description: Create clear 16:9 visuals for lessons, workshops, and explainers with strict text budgets.

use_cases.title: Batch Produce Prompt Packs
use_cases.description: Build repeatable prompt packs and optional APIMart request files for multiple related images.

prompt_templates.title: Plan One Infographic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-assistant for this text. Ask only the missing brief questions, then recommend one 16:9 infographic and its purpose.

prompt_templates.title: Compress Copy For A Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-assistant to create a Copy Spec for one 16:9 teaching slide. Keep each region to short Chinese text and list the exact words to place on the image.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Multi-Image Prompt Pack
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-assistant to split this article into three images. For each image, provide the purpose, layout template, Copy Spec, and final prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Batch API Requests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-assistant stage 4 for confirmed Copy Specs. Create a manual prompt pack first, then prepare APIMart batch request content only after I choose API mode.

output_examples.input: A product strategy article needs one summary visual for a presentation.
output_examples.output: The skill returns one 16:9 overview plan, a short Copy Spec, and a final prompt with layout, style, and readability constraints.

output_examples.input: A course outline has five modules and needs a cover image.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a roadmap layout with five large module titles, minimal extra text, and a consistent hand-drawn infographic style.

output_examples.input: Three confirmed prompts need production through APIMart.
output_examples.output: The skill prepares batch request content and points to the local script workflow, while warning that prompt content goes to the configured API.

best_practices: Confirm audience, viewing distance, and text density before asking for final prompts.
Use the Copy Spec as the only source of on-image text before entering prompt packaging.
Run API mode only with a trusted endpoint and non-sensitive prompt content.
anti_patterns: Do not put full paragraphs or dense explanations inside one image.
Do not skip Copy Spec confirmation before creating final prompts.
Do not store real API tokens in shared repositories or chat transcripts.

faq.question: Does this skill create image files by itself?
faq.answer: Not by default. It writes prompts and can optionally use a local APIMart batch script.

faq.question: What image format does it target?
faq.answer: It defaults to 16:9 horizontal infographic prompts unless the user asks for another format.

faq.question: Can it handle long articles?
faq.answer: Yes. It first splits content into image candidates, then narrows each image to one core message.

faq.question: Does it support English prompts?
faq.answer: Yes, but the included workflow and templates focus on readable Chinese infographic text.

faq.question: Is API generation safe?
faq.answer: API mode is optional. It sends prompt content to the configured service and requires careful token handling.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
